report_name: INC Ransomware Skyrockets to Top-Tier Threat, Claiming Over 830 Victims

threat_actors.name: INC Ransomware
threat_actors.description: A prolific ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that emerged in August 2023, compromising over 830 organizations. The group's rapid growth is fueled by attracting experienced affiliates from defunct groups like LockBit and BlackCat. They use a Rust-based encryptor and target critical sectors, particularly in the United States.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment and data exfiltration (double extortion).

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: A defunct or disrupted ransomware group from which experienced affiliates have reportedly migrated to the INC Ransomware operation.

threat_actors.name: BlackCat
threat_actors.description: A defunct or disrupted ransomware group from which experienced affiliates have reportedly migrated to the INC Ransomware operation.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: INC Ransomware affiliates use spear-phishing campaigns with malicious links or attachments to gain initial access to victim networks.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Affiliates exploited known, unpatched vulnerabilities in public-facing applications such as Citrix Netscaler (CVE-2023-3519), Fortinet EMS (CVE-2023-48788), and SimpleHelp (CVE-2024-57727) for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: After gaining entry, INC affiliates use custom credential dumpers to harvest credentials. An updated dumper was observed targeting newer Veeam backup deployments.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The group uses legitimate remote access software to move across the network and blend in with normal administrative activity.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack involves deploying the Rust-based INC Ransomware payload to encrypt files on Windows systems and Linux-based ESXi servers.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: INC Ransomware engages in double extortion by exfiltrating sensitive data before encryption and threatening to leak it on their dark web site.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The group uses commands such as 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' to delete Volume Shadow Copies, preventing system recovery from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: INC affiliates use encoded PowerShell commands for obfuscating malicious scripts used for discovery and lateral movement.

malware.name: INC Ransomware
malware.description: A cross-platform ransomware payload written in Rust, used to encrypt files on both Windows and Linux/ESXi environments. It appends the .inc extension to encrypted files.

tools.name: SimpleHelp
tools.description: A legitimate remote support software that was targeted via vulnerability CVE-2024-57727 for initial access.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool suggested for monitoring as part of hunting for INC Ransomware TTPs.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The Windows Volume Shadow Copy Service administrative command-line tool was used to delete shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used with encoding (`-enc`) to obfuscate and execute malicious scripts for discovery and lateral movement.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-3519
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Citrix Netscaler exploited by INC Ransomware affiliates to gain initial access.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-48788
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Fortinet EMS exploited by INC Ransomware affiliates to gain initial access.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2024-57727
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in SimpleHelp remote support software exploited by INC Ransomware affiliates to gain initial access.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: A primary target sector for the INC Ransomware group, where operational disruption creates immense pressure to pay ransoms.

identities.name: Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: A primary target sector for the INC Ransomware group.

identities.name: Legal Services Sector
identities.description: A primary target sector for the INC Ransomware group.

infrastructure.name: INC Ransomware Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the INC Ransomware group to threaten the release of data exfiltrated from victims as part of their double extortion strategy.

indicators.name: INC Ransomware VSS Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: INC Ransomware VSS Deletion
id: 7e8b1c2a-3d4e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete Volume Shadow Copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment observed with INC Ransomware.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/inc-ransomware-claims-830-victims-since.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows', a technique used by INC Ransomware to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: INC Ransomware Encoded PowerShell Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: INC Ransomware Encoded PowerShell Execution
id: 8f9c0d1e-2f3a-4b5c-6d7e-8f9a0b1c2d3e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of encoded PowerShell commands, a technique used by INC Ransomware affiliates for discovery and lateral movement.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/inc-ransomware-claims-830-victims-since.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects encoded PowerShell commands used by INC Ransomware for obfuscating malicious scripts.

indicators.name: INC Ransomware File Encryption
indicators.pattern:
title: INC Ransomware File Encryption Detected
id: 9a0b1c2d-3e4f-5a6b-7c8d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects files being renamed with the .inc extension, which is indicative of an active INC Ransomware encryption event.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/inc-ransomware-claims-830-victims-since.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.inc'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with the '.inc' extension, indicating active encryption by INC Ransomware.

indicators.name: Citrix Netscaler Path Traversal Attempt (CVE-2023-3519)
indicators.pattern:
title: Citrix Netscaler Path Traversal Attempt (CVE-2023-3519)
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-a7b8-c9d0-e1f2a3b4c5d6
status: experimental
description: Detects a URL pattern associated with exploiting Citrix Netscaler vulnerabilities, a TTP used by INC Ransomware affiliates.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/inc-ransomware-claims-830-victims-since.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/vpn/../..'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a URL pattern '/vpn/../..' which is a hunting hint for exploiting Citrix Netscaler, a method used by INC affiliates.
